mnemonics.title: Jordan Block Structure
mnemonics.content: Jordan Block = λ on diagonal, 1's climb the superdiagonal stairs - like steps going up!

mnemonics.title: AM vs GM
mnemonics.content: Algebraic is ALWAYS Greater (or equal) to Geometric - AM ≥ GM like Alphabetically A comes before G but A ≥ G for this rule!

mnemonics.title: Diagonalizable Condition
mnemonics.content: For Diagonalizable: AM = GM for EVERY eigenvalue - think Equal E's make diagonalizable!

mnemonics.title: Minimal Polynomial Degree
mnemonics.content: Min poly degree = Largest Jordan Block Size - Max block tells the power in min poly!

mnemonics.title: Finding Generalized Eigenvectors
mnemonics.content: Start with eigenvector v₁ where (A-λI)v₁=0, then find v₂ where (A-λI)v₂=v₁, then v₃ where (A-λI)v₃=v₂ - each step adds one more (A-λI)!

acronyms.usage: The standard form J = P⁻¹AP showing block diagonal structure
Count of eigenvalue repetitions in characteristic polynomial
Dimension of eigenspace = number of Jordan blocks for eigenvalue
Finding canonical representation: compute char poly → find eigenvalues → find chains → construct blocks

visualTips.visualization: Picture a staircase: diagonal has λ, λ, λ going down-right, superdiagonal has 1, 1 going up-right - like steps to eigenvalue heaven!
Think of dominoes falling: (A-λI)v₁=0 (standing), (A-λI)v₂=v₁ (hits first domino), (A-λI)v₃=v₂ (hits second) - one application creates chain!
Each Jordan block is like a mini-matrix town: eigenvalue λ in every house (diagonal), exactly one road (1 on superdiagonal) connecting neighbors!
AM = total area of eigenvalue's territory, GM = number of separate islands - you can't have more islands than total area!

analogies.analogy: A Jordan block is like an apartment building: eigenvalue λ is the building color, each floor (row) connects to floor above by staircase (1 on superdiagonal), and everyone in building shares same eigenvalue!
Like a relay race: eigenvector passes the baton (vector) to next runner (generalized eigenvector), each handoff is multiplication by (A-λI)!
Jordan Canonical Form is like DNA for matrices - it reveals the essential structural information (eigenvalues, block sizes) regardless of similarity transformation!
The nilpotent part N in J = λI + N is like the 'non-diagonal' energy - the I part gives stability (λ), N adds the 'cyclic' behavior within each block!
